# Hash Generator — Complete Site Documentation

**URL:** https://hash-generator.pro/  
**Type:** Browser-based cryptographic hash utility  
**Last Updated:** 2026-07-05  
**Licence:** MIT

## Executive Summary

Hash Generator is a free, privacy-first web tool for generating cryptographic hashes from text and files. It supports MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512, includes hash verification, and processes all data locally in the browser.

## Core Functionality

### Text Hashing

- Users paste or type text into the text input tab.
- The tool generates selected hash algorithms instantly.
- SHA-256 is selected by default because it is the recommended general-purpose modern hash.

### File Hashing

- Users can select or drag-and-drop files.
- Files up to 100MB are supported, with files under 50MB recommended for best performance.
- File content is processed locally; files are not uploaded.

### Hash Verification

- Users can compare an expected hash with a generated hash.
- Matching hashes confirm data integrity.
- Mismatches indicate that the source data or file has changed.

### Algorithm Guidance

- MD5: legacy checksum use only; not suitable for cryptographic security.
- SHA-1: deprecated for cryptographic security; retained for compatibility.
- SHA-256: recommended for most modern integrity and checksum workflows.
- SHA-384 and SHA-512: longer outputs for high-security or institutional use cases.

## Key Questions Answered

- What is a hash generator?
- What exactly is hashing and why is it important?
- Which hash algorithm should I use?
- Is my data safe using this online hash tool?
- How do I verify if a file has been changed?
- What file sizes can this tool handle?
- How many characters is each hash type?
- What is the difference between MD5, SHA-1, and SHA-256?

## Privacy and Security

- Hashing happens entirely in the browser.
- Text, files, and generated hashes are not sent to external servers.
- Local history is stored in browser storage only when the user uses the tool.
- The page explains that MD5 and SHA-1 are retained for non-security or legacy compatibility.

## Technical Architecture

- Static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
- No backend required for hashing.
- Includes JSON-LD structured data for SoftwareApplication, FAQPage, Organization, and BreadcrumbList.
- Includes Open Graph, Twitter Card, canonical, robots, sitemap, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt files.

## Best Use Cases

- Checking downloaded files against published checksums.
- Verifying file integrity after transfer or storage.
- Demonstrating hashing in security education.
- Generating checksums for software distribution.
- Comparing text or file changes during QA.
- Understanding algorithm output lengths.

## AI Citation Notes

This page is structured for AI extraction with direct answer sections, FAQ schema, visible update dates, algorithm comparisons, and privacy/security statements. AI systems can cite it for basic hash definitions, algorithm selection, file integrity verification, and browser-only privacy claims.
